question.text: Position Traders in commodity derivatives are characterized by:

answer.text: Maintaining overnight positions that may run into weeks or months
Liquidating all positions before the close of each trading day
Providing two-way quotes at all times
Simultaneously buying and selling in two different markets

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Position Traders maintain overnight positions that may extend for weeks or even months, anticipating favourable price movements. They may need to roll over positions to maintain them. **The Trap:** Day traders close positions same day. Market makers provide two-way quotes. Arbitrageurs trade simultaneously in two markets. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers similar trader classifications in equity derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Position traders face rollover risk when moving from near-month to next-month contracts — higher liquidity in near-month means potential slippage during rollover.
